channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Higher-order neuromorphic Ising machines—autoencoders and Fowler-Nordheim annealers are all you need for scalability
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 16 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-71937-4

The authors demonstrate that an autoencoder-based neuromorphic architecture combined with Fowler-Nordheim annealing, is sufficient to implement scalable higher-order Ising machines. They show that these machines can consistently produce state-of-the-art solutions with high reliability and competitive time-to-solution metrics.
item.dc:title: Higher-order neuromorphic Ising machines—autoencoders and Fowler-Nordheim annealers are all you need for scalability
item.dc:creator: Faiek Ahsan
Saptarshi Maiti
Zihao Chen
Jakob Kaiser
Ankita Nandi
Madhuvanthi Srivatsav
Johannes Schemmel
Andreas G. Andreou
Jason Eshraghian
Chetan Singh Thakur
Shantanu Chakrabartty
